NEW_LAW_001.description: Industrial Emissions Control Act: Unlawful to emit pollutants posing risks to public health. Facilities must maintain filters. Operating at 'Low Safety' or 'Medium Safety' for two consecutive inspection periods constitutes a violation.
NEW_LAW_001.penalty: Fine of $7,500 per violation. Compensation of $200 payable to affected parties.

ENV-001.description: Prohibits industrial emissions that fail to maintain at minimum 'Medium Safety' standards. Emissions meeting 'Medium Safety' are permitted provided they do not cause demonstrable, immediate harm to public health.
ENV-001.penalty: Fine of $1,000 per violation. Compensation of $500 payable to affected parties.

PUBLIC_NUISANCE.description: Unlawful to emit smoke observable as persistent haze within 2 miles for >1 hour. Violation established by 2 witnesses or visual evidence. Applies regardless of safety level, though safety level is considered for severity.

ENVIRONMENTAL_SAFETY.description: Requires filtration systems. 'Low Safety' (no filters) is prohibited. 'High Safety' operation earns a 50% rebate on nuisance fines for the same month.
ENVIRONMENTAL_SAFETY.penalty: Fine of $1,500 per violation. Compensation of $25 per affected resident.

NEW_LAW_001.description: Air Quality and Emissions Control Act: Unlawful to emit visible pollutants. Per se violation if: (1) Facility operates at 'Low Safety', OR (2) Visible pollutants are documented regardless of safety level.

ENV-001.description: Prohibits emissions resulting in a Factory Inspection Report Safety Level of 'Medium' or 'Low'. Compliance determined solely by official report data. Subjective resident observations inadmissible.
Prohibits emitting pollutants at levels that demonstrably harm public health. The standard for violation is a Safety Level rating of 'Low' or 'Medium' in the official report.

ENV-2025-001.description: Prohibits discharging pollutants beyond safety thresholds. Requires operational filtration. Failure to have a functional system is a severe violation. 'Low Safety' rating is prima facie evidence of violation.
ENV-2025-001.penalty: Fine of $5,000 per violation. Compensation of $1,000 payable to affected parties.

NEW_LAW_001.description: Factory Air Quality Standards Act: Unlawful to emit visible smoke causing measurable degradation. Facilities operating at Low Safety levels (no filters) are presumed to be in violation.
NEW_LAW_001.penalty: Fine of $2,500 per violation. Compensation of $500 payable to affected parties.

ENV-2025-001.description: Facilities must operate with basic filtration. Emissions must not create visible plumes. A 'Medium Safety' rating constitutes a violation of this standard.

ENV-2025-002.description: Residents suffering verifiable health impacts from a violation are entitled to compensation. Requires 'Low' or 'Medium' safety operation and medical documentation.
ENV-2025-002.penalty: Factory must pay $500 compensation per eligible resident per violation.

ENV-001.description: Unlawful to emit visible pollutants. 'Low Safety' rating is prima facie evidence of violation. 'Medium Safety' requires monitoring.
ENV-001.penalty: Fine of $1,000 per violation. Compensation of $200 payable to affected parties.

ENV-002.description: Unlawful to operate for more than two consecutive months with a 'Medium Safety' rating. Three consecutive months of 'Medium' or any month of 'Low' constitutes a violation.
ENV-002.penalty: Fine of $750 per violation. Compensation of $150 payable to affected parties.
